name: Apple Watch Series 10
verdict: The best smartwatch for iPhone users — FDA-cleared ECG, always-on display, crash detection, and the deepest iOS integration.
description: The Apple Watch Series 10 remains the smartwatch to beat for iPhone owners. The always-on Retina display is brighter and thinner than ever, and the S9 chip enables on-device Siri and double-tap gesture control. Health features include FDA-cleared ECG, blood oxygen monitoring, skin temperature sensing, and sleep apnea detection. Crash detection and fall detection can automatically call emergency services. The depth gauge and water temperature sensor make it swim-friendly. With watchOS 11, the Apple Watch is less a notification device and more a genuine health companion.
features: FDA-cleared ECG and blood oxygen monitoring
Crash detection and fall detection with emergency SOS
Water resistant to 50m with depth gauge
cons: Requires iPhone — doesn't work with Android

name: Samsung Galaxy Watch 7
verdict: The best smartwatch for Android — BioActive sensor, Wear OS 5, and seamless Galaxy ecosystem integration.
description: The Galaxy Watch 7 is Samsung's mainstream flagship and the best smartwatch for most Android users. The 3-in-1 BioActive sensor handles heart rate, ECG, and body composition analysis in one chip. Wear OS 5 with Samsung's One UI Watch overlay delivers smooth performance and access to the full Google Play Store. The sapphire crystal display is tough enough for daily wear, and the aluminum case keeps weight down. Samsung Health provides comprehensive fitness tracking with auto-workout detection, sleep coaching, and stress management. At $300, it undercuts the Apple Watch while matching most of its health features.
features: Wear OS 5 with Google Play Store
pros: BioActive sensor matches Apple Watch health features

name: Garmin Venu 3
verdict: The best smartwatch for fitness — up to 14 days of battery, advanced training metrics, and Garmin's legendary GPS accuracy.
description: The Garmin Venu 3 bridges the gap between fitness tracker and smartwatch better than anything else. The AMOLED display is vibrant and always-on, battery life reaches up to 14 days in smartwatch mode (vs 18 hours for Apple Watch), and Garmin's training features are unmatched — Training Readiness, Body Battery, HRV status, and nap detection. It also supports phone calls via Bluetooth speaker and mic. Garmin Connect is the most comprehensive fitness app available, with decades of data science behind its algorithms. If fitness tracking is your primary reason for wearing a smartwatch, nothing else comes close.
features: Up to 14-day battery life in smartwatch mode
Training Readiness and Body Battery scores
Built-in speaker and mic for phone calls
Garmin Coach with adaptive training plans
pros: Best fitness and training metrics available
Phone calls without pulling out your phone
cons: App ecosystem is limited vs Apple/Google
Garmin Connect app has a learning curve

name: Google Pixel Watch 3
verdict: The best Wear OS smartwatch — pure Google experience with Fitbit health tracking and the cleanest interface.
description: The Pixel Watch 3 is what you get when Google designs both the hardware and software. The circular AMOLED display is gorgeous, Wear OS runs buttery smooth on Google's custom chip, and Fitbit's health tracking (now fully integrated) provides comprehensive wellness data. Google Maps on your wrist is genuinely useful for navigation, and Google Wallet handles payments. The 41mm and 45mm size options fit more wrists than the one-size Galaxy Watch. Battery life hits 24 hours with always-on display — not great, but competitive with Apple Watch.
pros: Google Maps on wrist is killer

name: Apple Watch SE (2024)
verdict: The best budget Apple Watch — crash detection, fall detection, and core health tracking for $150 less than the Series 10.
description: The Apple Watch SE delivers 80% of the Series 10 experience at 60% of the price. You get the same S9 chip, crash detection, fall detection, heart rate monitoring, sleep tracking, and full watchOS 11 — just without the always-on display, ECG, and blood oxygen sensors. For most people, those omissions don't matter day-to-day. The SE is the perfect Apple Watch for first-time buyers, kids (with Family Setup), and anyone who wants iPhone integration without the premium price. At $249, it's the smartwatch sweet spot.
features: Family Setup for kids without iPhone
pros: Family Setup is unique to Apple

name: Garmin Forerunner 265
verdict: The best running watch — morning report, training readiness, and race predictor with 13-day battery life.
description: The Forerunner 265 is the watch serious runners swear by. The morning report greets you with sleep quality, HRV status, training readiness, and weather — everything you need to decide today's workout intensity. Race predictor estimates your 5K, 10K, half marathon, and marathon times based on your training data. The AMOLED display is new for the Forerunner line and looks stunning. 13-day battery in smartwatch mode (20 hours in GPS mode) means you charge it once every two weeks. If you run more than twice a week, this watch will make you faster.
features: Morning report with daily training readiness
pros: Best running watch on the market
cons: $450 is expensive for a running watch
Not as stylish as fashion smartwatches

name: Amazfit GTR 4
verdict: The best budget smartwatch — 14-day battery, GPS, and health tracking for $200 with both Android and iPhone support.
description: The Amazfit GTR 4 delivers flagship-level hardware at half the price of Apple and Samsung. The 1.43-inch AMOLED display is bright and responsive, battery life reaches 14 days, and the dual-band GPS is accurate for outdoor activities. Health tracking covers heart rate, blood oxygen, stress, and sleep with Amazfit's Zepp OS providing a clean interface. It works with both Android and iPhone — no ecosystem lock-in. The stainless steel case and leather/silicone band options make it look more expensive than it is. At $200, it's the best value smartwatch for people who want health tracking without the Apple/Samsung tax.

name: Apple Watch Ultra 2
verdict: The most rugged Apple Watch — titanium case, 36-hour battery, dual-frequency GPS, and built for extreme sports.
description: The Apple Watch Ultra 2 is built for people who push limits. The 49mm titanium case survives drops, impacts, and extreme temperatures that would destroy a regular Apple Watch. Dual-frequency GPS provides the most accurate location tracking Apple offers — critical for trail running, open-water swimming, and backcountry hiking. The 36-hour battery life (72 hours in low power mode) finally addresses Apple Watch's biggest weakness. The Action Button provides one-touch access to workouts, waypoints, or shortcuts. The 2000-nit display is readable in direct sunlight. If you need an Apple Watch that can survive anything, this is it.
cons: 49mm is too large for smaller wrists

name: Fitbit Charge 6
verdict: The best fitness tracker — slim band design, Google integration, and 7-day battery for people who want health tracking without a big watch.
description: Not everyone wants a chunky smartwatch on their wrist. The Fitbit Charge 6 is for people who want health and fitness tracking in a slim, comfortable band they can wear 24/7 without noticing. It tracks heart rate, sleep stages, stress, SpO2, and 40+ exercise types. Google integration means you get Google Maps, Google Wallet, and YouTube Music controls. The 7-day battery means weekly charging, not daily. The built-in GPS tracks outdoor runs and walks without your phone. At $160, it's the best fitness tracker for people who prioritize health data over smartwatch features.
features: Google Maps, Wallet, and YouTube Music
cons: Fitbit Premium required for some features

name: Withings ScanWatch 2
verdict: The best hybrid smartwatch — looks like a luxury analog watch, tracks health like a medical device, with 30-day battery.
description: The Withings ScanWatch 2 solves the biggest problem with smartwatches: they look like tech gadgets, not watches. The ScanWatch looks like a $500 analog timepiece — sapphire crystal, stainless steel case, real watch hands — but underneath it packs FDA-cleared ECG, blood oxygen monitoring, respiratory rate tracking, and sleep apnea detection. The small OLED display shows notifications and health data without ruining the classic aesthetic. 30-day battery life means you charge it once a month. For professionals who can't wear an Apple Watch to a boardroom, the ScanWatch is the answer.
features: Analog watch design with sapphire crystal
Withings Health Mate app with doctor sharing
pros: Looks like a luxury watch, not tech
cons: No app store or third-party apps
